fingerprint: 6.9 min = tan²(ε/2) × 720/π / √2 — the obliquity RMS is a spherical trigonometric projection of globe axial tilt
globe_value: tan²(23.44°/2) × (720/π) / √2 = 6.97 min (obliquity component RMS of equation of time)
why_it_matters: The value 6.9 min is not just 'cherry-picked' — it is algebraically traceable through spherical geometry that has no flat-disc analog. The derivation chain: (1) obliquity ε = 23.44° is the angle between Earth's rotational axis and orbital plane normal — undefined on a flat disc with a local sun; (2) the equation of time's obliquity component amplitude = tan²(ε/2) × 720/π = 9.86 min, where tan²(ε/2) is a spherical trigonometric coefficient arising from the ecliptic-to-equatorial coordinate transformation on a sphere; (3) RMS = 9.86/√2 = 6.97 min. The factor tan²(ε/2) specifically requires projecting one great circle (ecliptic) onto another (equator) using spherical trig — there is no geometric derivation of this factor from a flat disc. The dome borrows 23.45° directly from globe astronomy (confirmed by WIN-056's solar formula), and 6.9 min is a downstream consequence of that borrowed parameter passed through spherical projection mathematics.
derivation_chain: ε = 23.44° (globe axial tilt — undefined on a flat disc)
y = tan²(ε/2) = 0.04304 (spherical trig projection coefficient, ecliptic → equatorial)
suggested_text: The dome's 6.9-minute value is not merely cherry-picked — it is algebraically downstream of globe geometry. The equation of time's obliquity component has amplitude tan²(ε/2) × 720/π = 9.86 min, where ε = 23.44° is Earth's axial tilt and tan²(ε/2) is a spherical trigonometric projection coefficient with no derivation on a flat disc. RMS = 9.86/√2 = 6.97 min — the dome's '6.9.' The dome's own solar formula imports this 23.45° tilt from globe astronomy (see WIN-056), making this another case where a globe parameter, processed through spherical geometry, is relabeled as a dome 'prediction.'
notes: Moderately significant fingerprint. The chain globe-tilt → spherical-trig → 6.9 min is clean and complete. Less dramatic than the a = πR = 20,015 km fingerprint (WIN-003 family) but follows the same structural pattern: dome adopts a globe-derived number without being able to derive it from its own geometry. The tan²(ε/2) factor is particularly damning because it is specifically a spherical-geometry quantity — it arises from the difference between uniform motion along the equator vs. the ecliptic, which are two intersecting great circles on a sphere.
